PROJECT OVERVIEW:
This project involves developing an application with user authentication, password management, reporting functionalities, notifications, and communication between users. The application will also have performance and robustness measures, and it will be designed to work across different devices and environments. The application will utilize Firebase for backend services and will require a minimum internet speed of 5 Mb/s for optimal performance.

TEST CASES

Title: System Response Time for a Specific Feature
Test Steps:
1. Identify a specific feature that requires processing a certain amount of data and resources.
2. Measure the response time of the system when the feature is invoked.
3. Record the time taken for the system to respond and process the feature.
Expected Result: The response time should meet the predefined threshold specified in the requirements (RNF-03, RNF-04, RNF-07).

Title: Throughput Processing Time
Test Steps:
1. Determine the throughput required for a specific feature.
2. Measure the time taken to process the throughput.
3. Record the time taken for the system to handle the throughput.
Expected Result: The throughput processing time should meet the predefined threshold specified in the requirements (RNF-03, RNF-04, RNF-07).

Title: System Functionality Under Error Conditions
Test Steps:
1. Introduce an error condition into the system (e.g., unexpected input, edge-case scenario).
2. Observe the behavior of the system under the error condition.
3. Verify that the system continues to function as expected despite the error.
Expected Result: The system should continue to function correctly and provide appropriate error handling or fallback mechanisms as specified in the requirements (RNF-02, RNF-08, RNF-09, RNF-10, RNF-11).

Title: System Functionality Across Different Hardware Environments
Test Steps:
1. Deploy the application on different hardware environments (e.g., different versions of Android, different memory configurations).
2. Observe the behavior of the system in each environment.
3. Verify that the system functions as expected across all tested environments.
Expected Result: The system should function correctly and efficiently across all tested hardware environments as specified in the requirements (RNF-04).

Title: System Functionality in Edge-Case Scenarios
Test Steps:
1. Identify edge-case scenarios that could affect the system's functionality.
2. Simulate the edge-case scenarios in the system.
3. Observe the behavior of the system under these scenarios.
Expected Result: The system should handle edge-case scenarios gracefully and maintain its functionality as specified in the requirements (RNF-02, RNF-08, RNF-10, RNF-11). <|
